images.caption: Figure 1:Token distribution across sparse encoders.OtherandETCrepresent additional language and non-linguistic tokens, respectively.
Figure 2:Overview of the SemBridge embedding initialization process.

Figure 3:Training loss trajectories for Splade-v3 on Chinese, Korean, and Russian using Baseline, OFA, FOCUS, and SemBridge. Theyy-axis is Loss and thexx-axis is Epoch. Insets zoom in on the initial training stage (0-0.2 epoch)
Figure 4:Zero-shot retrieval performance (nDCG@10) on (a) WebFAQ and (b) MIRACL. For SemBridge, the sparsity levelα\alphais varied from 1 to 4, with FOCUS and OFA included as baselines.
Figure 5:Efficiency vs. performance trade-off on the MIRACL dev set (Chinese). The y-axis shows FLOPS, and marker color indicates nDCG@10.

Table 3:Qualitative comparison of top-weighted source tokens forsplade-v3initialization of target-language words corresponding to “home” across five languages. Tokens are listed in descending order of similarity for each method, and highlighted tokens indicate direct semantic equivalents and core matches for the concept of “home”.
